records.ety: [OF. guerdon, guerredon, LL. widerdonum (influenced by L. donum gift, cf. Donation ), fr. OHG. widarlōn; widar again, against (G. wider wieder) + lōn reward, G. lohn, akin to AS. len Goth. laun. See Withers.]

defs: A reward; requital; recompense; -- used in both a good and a bad sense.
quotes: So young as to regard men's frown or smile As loss or guerdon of a glorious lot.
He shall, by thy revenging hand, at once receive the just guerdon of all his former villainies.

defs: To give guerdon to; to reward; to be a recompense for.
quotes: Him we gave a costly bribe To guerdon silence.
